preferred_translation: all that has been put together is impermanent
definition: Canonical three-marks formula stating the impermanent character of saṅkhārā.
notes: Use this record for the full formula line. Dhp 277 anchors the line itself. Here the saṅkhārā family takes its result-side reading, so the line should sound like compounded or put-together things rather than the dependent-arising process of putting things together. The OSF glossary supports keeping the formula inside the repository's governed saṅkhārā family rather than defaulting to formations-language.

context_rules.rendering: all that has been put together is impermanent

context_rules.context: When prose needs a more idiomatic plural noun
context_rules.notes: Controlled alternate that still keeps the compounded side audible.

example_phrases.translation: all that has been put together is impermanent
example_phrases.notes: Keep saṅkhārā on the result-side reading in this line.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the canonical formula line itself.
Supports keeping the line inside the governed saṅkhārā family rather than defaulting to formations-language.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use as the standard rendering for the full Dhp 277 three-marks line.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not force dependent-arising process language into this formula slot, and do not restore formations as an unstated default.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the formula from drifting away from the governed result-side handling of saṅkhārā.
